body.title: SWADL: cross-platform BPA platform vision and roadmap
body.result: closed as noise per Akien review 2026-06-07
body.description:
SWADL is not just browser automation — it is a cross-platform Business Process Automation platform competitive with UiPath/Automation Anywhere/Blue Prism, with an AI-native learning layer those products lack. The buyer is the EM or ops director (pain: manual repetitive business processes), not the QA engineer (pain: brittle tests). The process recorder (spy-on-human) is the standard BPA onboarding UX. Self-healing is the retention moat.

Existing OS-level drivers: DogtailDriver (Linux, full), PyAutoGUI (cross-platform mouse/keyboard, full), PywinautoDriver (Windows, stub). Gap: macOS accessibility layer (PyObjC / Accessibility framework / AppleScript bridging). Wire Playwright as default browser driver (already implemented, not wired). Puppeteer: do not add.

Design session with Akien to cover: Mac OS layer implementation path; Windows stub completion priority; process recorder architecture (sync vs async clarification); LLM provider abstraction (add when needed, env-var switch first); monetization unit (seat vs workflow vs outcome); sales motion (bolt-on vs rip-and-replace); PII scrubbing policy for recorded sessions; boundary spec for self-healing vs semantic rewrite.

**Affected files:** devices/swadl/ (TBD), /home/akien/dev/src/swadl/SWADL/engine/swadl_cfg.py, swadl_driver.py
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** In scope: vision alignment, Mac OS layer path, Playwright wiring, monetization framing. Out of scope: implementation (that follows from design decisions).
**Completion criteria:** Design session produces: (1) Mac OS driver approach decided, (2) process recorder sync/async decided, (3) monetization unit and buyer decided, (4) sprint tickets filed for each implementation chunk.
